summary: Iteration 3 backend + frontend tests fully passed (59/59 pytest + all Playwright flows). Backend: 17 new-feature tests (leaderboard ordering/rank/percentile, notifications on enroll/grade/announcement/live-class + mark-all-read + auth guard, forgot-password → log-extracted token → reset → login-with-new-password + reused-token 400 + invalid-token 400 + short-password 400 + unknown-email still 200, batch-scoped live-class visibility across batch A / batch B / not-enrolled / global + dashboard upcoming-classes respects same filter + notification scoping) and regression (teacher analytics, both-role login, payments demo enrollment). Frontend: forgot-password-link on AuthPage → /forgot-password (forgot-email-input + forgot-submit-button + success state); /reset-password with no token shows reset-no-token error; student login → notifications-bell + notifications-panel open; leaderboard-test-{id} link opens Leaderboard page with title + rows + podium; teacher login → notifications-bell + Live Classes schedule-class form exposes class-course-select + class-batch-select; live-class list respects student scope. Resend email delivery is in testing mode as documented — send attempts logged; no 500s observed.
critical_code_review_comments: notify.notify() launches asyncio.create_task(send_email(...)) inside a loop without awaiting or tracking — if the request-scoped event loop ends before delivery, the coroutine may be cancelled. Works today because FastAPI worker keeps the loop alive, but consider a background queue or asyncio.gather for reliability.

student_class_query() builds an $or that includes {'course_id': None} AND {'course_id': {'$exists': False}} — good, covers legacy docs. However there's no protection against a live_classes.batch_id being set while course_id is null (impossible via API but possible via DB tampering); the visibility check would then bypass batch filtering. Not urgent.

forgot_password logs the full reset link at INFO level to backend.err.log — this is intentional for dev/testing (per problem statement) but ensure it is toggled off / demoted to DEBUG in production to avoid credential leakage in logs.
notifications endpoint returns full 30 items without pagination — fine for MVP, but consider cursor/limit params before scale.
notify.notify()'s query {'_id': {'$in': user_ids}} on db.users works for string ids (uuids). Verified. No ObjectId leaks in any response (all pop _id → id).
test_credentials: Teacher: teacher@jamacademy.com / Teacher@123 · Student: student@jamacademy.com / Student@123 (both untouched). Forgot-password test uses throwaway test_forgot_* accounts, seeded credentials NOT modified.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed data (courses, tests, live classes, announcements, assignments) and delete them in finally blocks. Fresh students are created dynamically (test_it3_* / test_forgot_*) — they remain in the DB but do not affect any user-visible flows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 4 new features (leaderboard, notifications + bell, forgot/reset password, batch-scoped live classes) work end-to-end. Backend tests are split into 3 files: backend_test.py (iter 1), test_new_features.py (iter 2 — payments/batches/files/course-linked), test_iteration3.py (iter 3 — leaderboard/notifications/reset/scoped-classes/analytics). Reset-password extracts token by grepping /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log for 'Password reset link'. Resend runs in TESTING MODE — email delivery to non-owner addresses will fail silently at Resend API level; send_email() catches and logs. No 500s. Notification bell polls every 30s but also fetches on mount, so reload after actions if you want fresh count.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Iterations 1, 2 and 3 all pass. Payment gateway remains intentionally MOCKED (demo mode) and Resend email delivery is intentionally scoped to account owner in TESTING MODE per problem statement.
